title: Kevin Shortsleeve
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Kevin Shortsleeve
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

biography: Barnstable High School: Cape Cod. BFA, Emerson College, MA, University of Florida and D.Phil, Oxford. Author of several children's books and academic publications.
education_entries: Ph D in English Literature, Oxford University (England)
MA in English, University of Florida
research_interests: Literary Nonsense, Walt Disney, Dr. Seuss, Edward Gorey & Popular Culture.
teaching_interests: Children's Literature, The History of Children's Literature, Literary Nonsense, Fantasy, Fairy Tales, Writing for Children, Picture Books, Children's Poetry & Film.
publications: Research Report (2021). Edward Gorey Research Bibliography . Gale Literary Criticism Series. Volume, 246. Issue, 2021. Pages, 1 - 80.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2021). Nonsense (expanded 'World' edition) . New York University Press. Pages, 133-136.
Magazine/Trade Publication (2020). Endless Summers at Craigville Beach . Cape Cod Life Magazine. Volume, June/July 2020. Issue, June/July 2020. Pages, 8.
(2018). “Curiouser and Curiouser! The Role of Curiosity in Children’s Literature” . Oklahoma Humanities Magazine. Volume, Fall 2018. Pages, 28-29.
(2018). “John, William and Alice in Wonderland: The Children’s Literature that Inspired John Lennon” . Oklahoma Humanities Magazine. Volume, Fall 2018. Pages, 26-27.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2018). “Edward Gorey, Nonsense, Surrealism and Silent Matter” . Princteon University Press. Pages, 100-131.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2015). Nonsense, Magic, Religion and Superstition . Bookbird: A Journal of International Children's Literature. Volume, 3. Issue, 2015. Pages, 28-36.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2011). The Cat in the Hippie: Dr. Seuss, Nonsense, the Carnivalesque and the Sixties Rebel . Oxford University Press. Pages, 20.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2004). The Wonderful World of the Depression: Disney, Despotism and the 1930s . The Lion and the Unicorn, Johns Hopkins University Press. Volume, 28. Issue, January 2004. Pages, 1-30.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2002). Edward Gorey, Children’s Literature and Nonsense Verse . The Children's Literature Association Quarterly, Johns Hopkins University Press. Volume, 27. Issue, 1. Pages, 27-39.
